title: Noisy No More
description: A mod that adds additional volume settings
body:
# Noisy No More

**Noisy No More** is a client-side Minecraft mod that adds extra sliders to control the volume of specific, often loud sound sources — like pistons, dispensers, villagers, and enderman. It also introduces an optional volume model that automatically lowers the combined volume when multiple identical sounds play at once.

## Features
- **Volume sliders** for noisy blocks and entities like pistons, dispensers, villagers, enderman, and more.
- **Alternative volume model** with two modes:
  - **Off** — uses the default Minecraft sound system.
  - **Only mobs** — reduces volume only for mobs.
- **Client-side only** — works in singleplayer or on any server without needing server installation.
- **Future backports** — planned support for older Minecraft versions (coming later).

## How to configure
To access the settings, go to:  
**Options → Music & Sounds → Additional Sound Options**

## Dependencies
- [Fabric API](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api)

## Installation
1. Install [Fabric](https://fabricmc.net/use/) and [Fabric API](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api).
2. Download **Noisy No More** from Modrinth or [GitHub](https://github.com/Kurasava/noisy-no-more).
3. Put the `.jar` file into your Minecraft `mods` folder.
4. Launch the game using the Fabric profile.

---

🎧 Make Minecraft sound exactly the way you want — no more noisy surprises!
